evidence: We use artificial intelligence and other quantitative analysis tools and models, developed by us and third-party service providers.
Some of our competitors may be more successful than us in the development and implementation of new technologies... including services and platforms based on artificial intelligence.

We use artificial intelligence and other quantitative analysis tools and models, developed by us and third-party service providers. Such technology, analysis and modeling are highly complex and subject to limitations and risks.
Any changes from the use of artificial intelligence could potentially disrupt... our business models, investment strategies, investment performance, operational processes.

If the data we, or third parties whose services we rely on, use in connection with the development or deployment of artificial intelligence is incomplete, inadequate or biased... the performance of our products, services, and businesses could suffer.

Our personnel or the personnel of our service providers could, without our knowledge, improperly utilize or misappropriate artificial intelligence and machine-learning technology... including relating to the entry of confidential information into a technology platform.

The European Union has adopted Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, which establishes a comprehensive, risk-based regulatory framework governing the development, marketing, deployment and use of artificial intelligence systems.

limitations: No quantified AI investment amounts, headcount, or named AI programs disclosed in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 2 based on generic acknowledgment of AI tool usage without named programs or dollar figures.
AI narrative is largely risk-framed rather than use-case or deployment-framed; no deployed initiatives with operating detail or metrics are described.
Excerpts are heavily weighted toward risk disclosures; business/strategy sections describing AI investment programs, if any, may not have been captured in the pre-filtered excerpts.
